title: Animal Cell
description: Microscopic view of a typical animal cell.
text: Animal cells are the basic units of animal life. They contain a nucleus, cytoplasm, and specialized structures called organelles that perform important functions.

title: Blood Smear
description: Prepared blood smear showing blood cells.
text: Blood contains red blood cells that transport oxygen, white blood cells that help fight infections, and platelets that assist in blood clotting.

title: Cell Division
description: Cells undergoing the process of division.
text: Cell division allows organisms to grow, repair damaged tissues, and reproduce. During mitosis, one cell divides into two genetically identical cells.

title: Human 16-Cell Embryo
description: Early-stage human embryo consisting of sixteen cells.
text: A 16-cell embryo is an early stage of human development. The cells continue to divide and specialize, eventually forming different tissues and organs.

title: Mouth Epithelial Cells
description: Cells collected from the inner lining of the mouth.
text: Mouth epithelial cells form a protective layer inside the mouth. They are easy to observe under a microscope and are commonly used in biology classes.

title: Onion Epithelial Cells
description: Epidermal cells from an onion skin.
text: Onion cells are plant cells with clearly visible cell walls. They are often used to teach students about plant cell structure.

title: Paramecium
text: Paramecium is a microscopic organism that lives in freshwater. It uses tiny hair-like structures called cilia to move and gather food.

title: Plant Cell
description: Microscopic view of a typical plant cell.
text: Plant cells contain a cell wall, chloroplasts, and a large central vacuole. These structures help plants make food and maintain their shape.

title: Plant Leaf
text: Leaves are the primary sites of photosynthesis. Specialized cells in leaves capture sunlight and convert it into chemical energy.

title: Plant Tissue
description: Microscopic view of plant tissue structures.
text: Plant tissues are groups of cells that work together to perform specific functions such as support, transport of water, and food storage.

title: Prepared Slide Sample
description: Example of a prepared microscope slide.
text: Prepared slides are specimens mounted on glass slides for observation under a microscope. They allow students to study biological structures safely and clearly.
